economic-dispatch.note: within-limits criterion always fails due to testPattern 'within limits' not matching test name 'all generators within min/max limits' — max achievable is 80/100

changes_made.description: Added CRITICAL validation sections for angles (<1.0 rad) and generator limits. Increased lambda iteration tolerance (1e-6) and max iterations (200). Added explicit step-by-step implementation guidance.
Added complete Python scripts with validation assertions. Emphasized rounding outputs to avoid floating-point noise.

Added per-unit conversion guidance (S_base=100 MVA) — the critical fix for power-flow. When reactances are per-unit and loads are in MW, loads must be divided by S_base before B-matrix solve, then flows converted back to MW. Also added realistic example angles in output format section.

key_findings: Power-flow angles were 100x too large because model solved with MW loads directly against per-unit reactances. Per-unit conversion (divide by S_base=100) fixes this.

Economic-dispatch within-limits criterion has a benchmark bug: testPattern 'within limits' doesn't match test name 'all generators within min/max limits' (regex substring mismatch). The model's output is actually correct.
qwen3.5-397b follows Python code examples closely — providing complete, copy-paste-ready scripts is more effective than pseudocode.
